articles.title: LPA to enter PAR within 24 hours, easterlies to bring cloudy skies
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines – One of two low-pressure areas is likely to enter the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) within the next 24 hours, the weather bureau said Tuesday. One LPA is near the boundary of PAR and has a slim chance of developing into a tropical cyclone, Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) weather specialist Veronica Torres said. The other LPA, she said, is still far and is forecast to enter PAR this weekend. READ: ITCZ outside PAR, may intensify into LPA – Pagasa This LPA has a high chance of developing into a tropical depression in the next [...]... articles.title: HEAT INDEX WATCH: 15 PH areas forecast to reach danger level on May 5
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — Danger-level heat indices are forecast in 15 areas across the country on Tuesday, May 5, the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) said. Danger-level heat indices range from 42oC to 51oC and carry increased risks of heat cramps and heat exhaustion, which may lead to heat stroke, according to Pagasa.

articles.title: Fixing mega-leaks of methane would hugely boost energy stock amid crisis, report says
articles.description: International Energy Agency analysis shows methane leaks remained at near-record highs in 2025 Methane emissions from the energy sector remained at near record levels in 2025, the International Energy Agency concluded in a report. Tackling the emissions could make billions of cubic metres of gas available to international markets, a top priority as the war in the Middle East squeezes energy supplies , according to the report. articles.title: Damage reported across Eastern Visayas after magnitude 6.0 quake
articles.description: TACLOBAN CITY — Damage was reported in several parts of Eastern Visayas after a magnitude 6.0 earthquake struck off San Julian, Eastern Samar, on Monday. The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology has downgraded the magnitude of the quake from 6.1 to 6.0. READ: Eastern Samar suspends work, classes after magnitude 6.1 quake In San Julian, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office immediately conducted rapid assessments near the epicenter to determine possible structural damage. In Maydolong, the municipal government temporarily suspended water supply after a drop in water pressure triggered by the tremor. Technical personnel are currently inspecting [...]... articles.title: Trump administration falls behind on wildfire prevention with risky fire season ahead

articles.title: SMDC Nature opens Visayas Hub in Iloilo, deepening its regional presence
articles.description: SM Development Corporation (SMDC) has opened its SMDC Nature Visayas Hub in Iloilo City, marking a significant step in the company’s continued expansion beyond Metro Manila and its commitment to serve more homebuyers in key regional centers. Located at SM Strata Tower 2 along Benigno Aquino Avenue, the new hub provides a dedicated regional space for SMDC Nature, the company’s segment focused on creating more balanced, livable, and thoughtfully designed residential communities. More than a sales office, the Visayas Hub serves as SMDC Nature’s regional anchor in Western Visayas. It operates as a full-service hub with customer service and back-end [...]...
